title: EEG data during three near-threshold visual detection tasks: a no-cue task, a noninformative cue task (50% validity), and an informative cue task (100% validity)

description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from participants performing three near-threshold visual detection tasks with varying levels of attentional cueing: a no-cue baseline condition, a noninformative cue task with 50% validity, and an informative cue task with 100% validity. The study investigates the neural mechanisms underlying visual perception at detection threshold, with particular focus on the relationship between prestimulus alpha-band oscillations and perceptual performance.
methods_description: EEG data were collected during three near-threshold visual detection tasks: a no-cue task, a noninformative cue task (50% validity), and an informative cue task (100% validity). Participants performed perceptual detection of visual stimuli presented at near-threshold intensity levels. Analysis focused on the relationship between prestimulus alpha-band oscillations and perceptual performance.
